content:
# Recommendations — after iter-046 (HARD STOP / ESCALATION)

## TOP PRIORITY — ESCALATE the gap-fill out of the terminal-file lane
The terminal route is **not advanceable by another single-file prover round.** The shared keystone
`conjugateEquiv_restrictFunctorComp_inv` (restrict-side mirror of Mathlib `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`)
is **irreducible inside `TensorObjInverse.lean`**: its only section computation `whnf`-bombs the
`SheafOfModules.pushforward` carrier diamond in real `lake build` (verified 4× incl. maxHeartbeats 1600000 +
respectTransparency false), and there is no abstract shortcut because `restrictFunctorComp` is NOT defined as
`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (unlike `pullbackComp`). B2, B1-crux, and S2/S4b/telescope all reduce to it.

**Two out-of-lane routes (assign ONE):**
1. **(Preferred) Mathlib/root `leftAdjointCompIso` anchor.** Add to `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean`
   (or root `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`) the fact that `restrictFunctorComp f g` IS the `leftAdjointCompIso` of the
   composite restrict-adjunction (mirroring how `pullbackComp` is defined). Then
   `conjugateEquiv_restrictFunctorComp_inv` holds by `rfl` (via `conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv`) — no
   carrier-diamond computation at all. This is a `refactor` / `mathlib-build` lane.
2. **Root section proof.** Prove the lemma in `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` where the
   `SheafOfModules.pushforward` carrier diamond is the *home* defeq and the root already drives sorry-free
   section computations (`simp (zeta := false)` + `erw`, memory `k1-carrier-diamond`). Re-export, consume in
   `TensorObjInverse.lean`.

**Do NOT** re-dispatch a plain terminal-file prover on B1/B2/the gap-fill — it will reproduce the identical
whnf bomb. The progress-critic-046 made this an explicit hard stop.

## DO-NOT-RETRY (blocked, same approach)
- `conjugateEquiv_restrictFunctorComp_inv` via in-file section computation (any heartbeat ceiling / transparency
  setting). Carrier-diamond whnf bomb; route 1/2 above is the only path.
- B2 `restrictFunctorIsoPullback_comp_compat`, B1-crux `H1inv_app_eq_pullbackVal_restrict` — downstream of the
  gap-fill; do not re-assign as terminal-file tasks until it lands.

## Stale-comment cleanup (next prover that touches the file — review cannot edit `.lean`)
- L405–411 (lean-auditor major): B1 docstring still calls the `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` privacy blocker OPEN +
  "See `TO_USER.md`" — RESOLVED iter-045 (B1 body sorry-free). Prune to avoid false action-item propagation.
- L748/753/759 (minor): `exists_tensorObj_inverse` docstring references stale iter-206/218/226 numbering epoch.

## Blueprint coverage debt (planner — author prose; review does not)
- **NEW (major, blueprint-as-failure):** `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_restrictFunctorComp_inv`
  (TensorObjInverse.lean L216, `private`) — no blueprint block. Add `\label`+`\lean`+`\uses`(conjugateEquiv,
  restrictAdjunction unit/counit app, restrictFunctorComp/pushforwardComp hom_app, Opensᵒᵖ uniqueness) AND a
  Mathlib dependency anchor for `leftAdjointCompIso` / `conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv` so route 1 is
  blueprinted. (A `% NOTE:` documenting the obstruction was added to the B2 proof block this review.)
- **minor:** `H1inv_app_eq_pullbackVal_restrict` (L324) — referenced only in B1 proof prose; give it a `\lean{}`
  pin / its own block.
- Coverage debt unchanged otherwise: `archon dag-query unmatched` = 106 `lean_aux` nodes (generic plumbing,
  deferred consistently since iter-045). The two above are the only *new* / actionable additions this iter.

## Closest-to-completion (once gap-fill lands)
B1 body is already sorry-free; B2 + B1-crux reduce to the single gap-fill via the `b2mate045.md` recipe. After
the gap-fill, expect B2/B1-crux to close, then S2/S4b → telescope `trivialisation_restrict_compat`. The route is
ONE lemma from cascading — but that lemma is structurally out-of-lane.
